cross_scene_arcs.title: Artistic Integrity vs. Commercial Success
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Holden resists commercialization of his work, first facing public ridicule for his comic and later opposing the M-TV cartoon adaptation, prioritizing artistic credibility over financial gain.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: co-creator of a commercially released comic
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: conflict between artistic self-worth and commercial validation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Idealization and Emotional Rejection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Holden develops deep feelings for Alyssa, believing in a mutual connection, but struggles with her past and non-normative views on sex, leading to a confession, rejection, and final breakup after an inappropriate proposal.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Holden is rejected and emotionally isolated
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Holden struggles to understand love without control or conditions

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Sexual Double Standards
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Holden is repeatedly challenged to examine his discomfort with Alyssa's sexual history, particularly with men, revealing his internalized double standards despite a progressive self-image.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: confronted with non-normative views on sex and identity
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: exposed to critique of male sexual entitlement
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: struggles to reconcile his progressive self-image with his visceral discomfort about Alyssa's sexual history with men

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fracture and Isolation in Friendship
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Holden's declaration of love for Alyssa and opposition to the cartoon deal strain his bond with Banky, leading to emotional distance and isolation as shared values erode.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Holden and Banky remain connected but emotionally distant
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: emotional distance persists without resolution or reconciliation
